extraction.one_line_summary: VIVIANI Elia won from deep in the bunch after DRUCKER Jempy's 1100m-out attack shattered the sprint trains, forcing late improvisation.
tactical_patterns: DRUCKER Jempy's 1100m suicide attack forced all leadout trains to abandon structure, benefiting freelancers like VIVIANI.
Lotto-Jumbo starting sprint at 1500m allowed their train to be disrupted before the critical final 800m zone.
BONIFAZIO Niccolò positioned at back of bunch with 2km remaining suggests Bora-Hansgrohe misjudged transition from positioning to sprint phase.
MODOLO Sacha's team EF blocked road left side in final 1200m, deliberately choking positioning lanes for rivals.

rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: positive: won despite no visible leadout, showing exceptional late positioning instinct when organized trains collapsed
rider_signals.DRUCKER Jempy: positive: 1100m attack tactically astute even in defeat, destroyed all rival sprint structures when leadouts most vulnerable
rider_signals.VAN POPPEL Danny: negative: Lotto-Jumbo train initiated at 1500m but faded badly, suggesting either rider weakness or team misjudged effort distribution
rider_signals.BONIFAZIO Niccolò: negative: still at back of peloton at 2km mark despite teammates working, reveals positioning deficit in chaotic sprints
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: negative: completely invisible in finale despite being among favorites, suggests Bora-Hansgrohe's positioning failure was systemic
rider_signals.MODOLO Sacha: neutral: EF Education's blocking tactics worked but MODOLO couldn't capitalize, indicating team tactics outperformed rider form
